title: Stable Actor Pattern Refactor for All GET Query Hooks

requirements.text: Create a `useStableActor` hook in `frontend/src/hooks/useStableActor.ts` that accepts the raw actor from `useActor()`, stabilizes the reference using `useRef` so it only updates once when the raw actor first becomes available, and returns both the stable actor instance and an `isActorFetching` boolean status. This prevents downstream React Query effects from re-running due to raw actor reference changes.

quotes: Create a reusable hook that stabilizes the actor reference
Actor only updates once when raw actor becomes available
Prevents downstream effects from raw actor reference changes

acceptanceCriteria: Hook is created at `frontend/src/hooks/useStableActor.ts`
Stable actor reference does not change after initial assignment even if raw actor reference changes
Returns `{ stableActor, isActorFetching }` with correct types
Does not modify `useActor` hook or any protected files

requirements.text: Create a generic `useStableActorQuery` hook in `frontend/src/hooks/useStableActorQuery.ts` that wraps React Query's `useQuery`. It must: accept a selector function `(actor) => Promise<T>`, a `queryKey` array, and an optional `options` object; use `useStableActor` internally; only enable the query when the stable actor is available (`enabled: !!stableActor`); merge `isActorFetching` with the query's own `isLoading` so the returned `isLoading` is `true` during both actor initialization and query fetching; and return the full React Query result object with the enhanced `isLoading`.

quotes: Generic wrapper around React Query's useQuery
Only enables query when stable actor is available
Merges actor fetching state with query loading state
Returns complete query result with enhanced loading state

acceptanceCriteria: Hook is created at `frontend/src/hooks/useStableActorQuery.ts`
Query does not fire when stable actor is not yet available
Returned `isLoading` is `true` while actor is initializing even if React Query itself considers loading complete
Accepts and forwards all standard React Query `useQuery` options (staleTime, gcTime, retry, etc.)
TypeScript generics are used so return data type is inferred from the selector function

requirements.text: Refactor all public page GET query hooks to use `useStableActorQuery` while maintaining identical external interfaces. The affected hooks and their files are: `useGetAllCategories` and `useGetHomepageCategories` (homepage/side panel), `useHomepageAutocomplete` in `frontend/src/hooks/useHomepageAutocomplete.ts`, `useGetCategoryById` (category page), `useCategoryProductsInfinite` in `frontend/src/hooks/useCategoryProductsInfinite.ts` (note: uses `useInfiniteQuery` — adapt pattern accordingly), `useGetProduct` and `useGetProductPhoto` in `frontend/src/hooks/useProductQueries.ts`, and `useGetBothStoreDetails` / `useBothStoreDetails` in `frontend/src/hooks/useBothStoreDetails.ts`. For each hook: replace direct `useActor()` + `enabled: !!actor` pattern with `useStableActorQuery`; define a named selector function for the actor method; preserve all existing query keys, `staleTime`, `gcTime`, retry settings, and error handling; keep all Spanish-language error messages unchanged.

quotes: Refactor All Public Page GET Query Hooks
Maintain exact same return structure for backward compatibility
Preserve all existing query configurations (staleTime, gcTime, etc.)
Preserve all error messages in Spanish

acceptanceCriteria: All listed public page GET hooks no longer call `useActor()` directly for enabling queries
Each hook defines a dedicated named selector function (e.g., `selectAllCategories`)
`useCategoryProductsInfinite` and `useHomepageCategoriesInfinite` correctly adapt the pattern for `useInfiniteQuery` (stable actor gating via enabled flag using `useStableActor`)
Components consuming these hooks require zero changes
No duplicate API calls on component re-renders (verifiable via network tab)
All Spanish error messages remain unchanged
Cache invalidation continues to work as before

requirements.text: Refactor all admin page GET query hooks to use the `useStableActorQuery` pattern while maintaining identical external interfaces. The affected files include: `frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts` (category CRUD get hooks), `frontend/src/hooks/useProductQueries.ts` (admin product get hooks), `frontend/src/hooks/useSaleItemQueries.ts` (sale item get hooks), `frontend/src/hooks/useStoreDetailsQueries.ts` (store details get hooks), `frontend/src/hooks/useAdminUserManagement.ts` (user list get hook), `frontend/src/hooks/useAdminExport.ts` (export stats get hook), `frontend/src/hooks/usePublicCategories.ts`, `frontend/src/hooks/useCategoryProductsPaginated.ts`, and `frontend/src/hooks/useProductSearchForSales.ts`. For each GET query hook in these files: replace the direct `useActor()` + `enabled: !!actor` pattern with `useStableActorQuery` (or stable actor gating for `useInfiniteQuery` variants); define named selector functions; preserve all query keys, cache settings, and Spanish error messages.

quotes: Refactor all admin page GET query hooks

acceptanceCriteria: All GET query hooks in admin hook files use `useStableActorQuery` or stable actor gating
Mutation hooks (create, update, delete) are not modified
Admin pages (CategoriesPage, ProductsPage, OnSaleProductsPage, StoreDetailsPage, UserManagementPage, ExportPage) continue to function identically
No duplicate API calls in admin pages
All Spanish error messages preserved
Cache invalidation after mutations continues to work correctly

requirements.text: Ensure `useHomepageCategoriesInfinite` in `frontend/src/hooks/useHomepageCategoriesInfinite.ts` and `useCategoryProductsInfinite` in `frontend/src/hooks/useCategoryProductsInfinite.ts` are adapted for the stable actor pattern using `useStableActor` to gate the `enabled` option on `useInfiniteQuery`, since `useStableActorQuery` wraps `useQuery` only. The stable actor reference must be obtained via `useStableActor`, passed directly into the `queryFn`, and the query enabled only when `!!stableActor`. All existing pagination behavior, `getNextPageParam`, flattened page data, and `fetchNextPage` must be preserved.

quotes: useCategoryProductsInfinite - Category products with infinite scroll
No duplicate API calls on re-renders

acceptanceCriteria: Both infinite query hooks use `useStableActor` to stabilize actor reference
Infinite scroll pagination continues to work correctly
`fetchNextPage`, `hasNextPage`, and `isFetchingNextPage` remain functional
No duplicate API calls when component re-renders
Existing flattened page data structures are preserved

constraints: This is a frontend-only change — no backend modifications allowed
Do not modify any files listed in `frontend.immutablePaths`
All Spanish-language error messages must remain unchanged
Mutation hooks (create, update, delete) must not be refactored
All existing external hook interfaces must remain identical — consuming components must require zero changes
nonGoals: Refactoring mutation hooks (useCreate*, useUpdate*, useDelete*, useToggle*)
Changes to component files that consume these hooks
Adding new product features or UI changes
Changing query key naming conventions beyond what is needed for stability
